reviews.review_text: Mel at the front desk very friendly & good customer service, also a good guide, gave us good tips about finding food & travel.

Quite.
Not far from shops.
Beaches not far.
Has a pool.
Stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin.
Clean tidy cabin with everything you need Park clean and tidy shop full of goodies staff friendly and helpful we will be back
Great place to stay with the dog. Neat and tidy 2 room accommodation with kitchen and bathroom.
Great group of people who will do what they can to assist you in any way possible
Very clean place and a great price.
Noisy highway

They promote a play area for children that is why we booked in the first place. I have 2 under 3yrs of age. we arrive here and there is no updates on the website. Its been closed due getting some maintenance and I see nothing has been started. If we can get our money back n find another place that does have what it promotes I honestly would jus so I dont see my baby boys sad face when he sees that play ground. He was devastated and its disgusting and disappointing that noone informed us of any closures of any facilities. Our TV wasn't working so no TV the doors were off the hinges so my baby boy almost went flying through  the sliding door . They need to fix a few things.
But in saying that they did give us a refund for one night after I complained which i thought  was fair. The room was clean

Banana Coast Caravan Park is a fun Park for children. The park is beautifully maintained, peaceful, and very well organised. The amenities are clean and thoughtfully designed, and the staff are warm, welcoming, and always helpful. Its convenient location makes it easy to explore the surrounding area like Byron Bay. A perfect spot for families, couples, or anyone looking for a pleasant and hassle free evening. Highly recommended. This park closes at 5 PM.

We're staying in one of their dulux cabins overnight on our way to Sydney. Its by no means the Ritz, but its certainly tidy and clean inside. The cabins look tired but hay, their not charging  the earth for accommodation. They have a nice pool, play area for the kids, and BBQ area. Plus if mentioned at time of booking, they also accommodate behaved dogs 🐶. The lady at re eption was nice & chatty (sorry, but I forgot to ask her name) all in all I've got no qualms in recommending the place. Cheers Ann 🌺
I stayed for one night . The cabin 1 . sofa smelled like dog wee.  Loud trucks going past all night .  Never staying again .

For an older style caravan park it provided great budget accommodation. The staff were amazing people and very friendly. Very close to the big banana and other activities. Could not fault for what we paid.

Five Stars for Mel!!

This was the third Holiday Park I called, and the phone was answered right away, Mel was only two happy to assist. I explained we were road tripping to Sydney with some family and travelling with a couple of medium doggos and we needed a place to stop for the night. Mel ran me through all our options and it was just simple and easy, I booked and paid on the spot!!

Don't bother calling Saffire Beach Holiday Park, they are rude and don't like dogs.. and Reflections didn't answer the phone. Call Mel @ Bananacoast, she is great and was a joy to chat with over the phone.

Not super fancy but nothing wrong with anything. We were comfortable and appreciated the kitchen. We did not have any wifi while there - was told it was the storm. Be aware if U must have wifi that it can be unreliable. No idea if that's a local issue or an issue for the caravan park
It was value for money and cabins clean staff helpful when making booking
Great place nice people
Cabins need a little tlc but are spotlessly clean. The service is amazing and the staff were very welcoming. Would definitely stay again.
The cabin was presented very clean and the staff were very helpful. Unfortunately the park is in need of refurbishment and the road noise from trucks and cars very loud.
Nice and clean quiet  when it’s so close to hwy

I have nothing but complimentary things to say about this Caravan park . My large family and I have stayed here quite a few times during our stop overs on the way to queensland and I can say the staff , the rooms and the facilities are excellent. The staff are so friendly welcoming and kind . On our last trip we stayed here over night on our way home myself, my husband and our 6 children . The staff accommodated us by placing us in cabins connected by a shared enclosed balcony which was perfect for a family sit down pizza night :) After we left and made it home my 8 year old son realized he had left something behind in the cabins we stayed in that he purchased from the gift shop at SeaWorld . I sent a message to the staff here and a beautiful lady named Shiralee replied saying that she would have cleaners look for my sons necklace . Than I received a message from another beautiful lady named Mel saying they had found my sons necklace and had posted it to my address . We received the necklace within days and my sons face when I showed him was priceless . He made sure I knew to thank the beautiful staff here and told me mum we HAVE to always stay at this place when we visit coffs Harbour :) Banana coast Caravan park you have won the heart of my youngest son and the respect and loyalty of my family . Thank you and we will see you guys again next year (booked rooms as soon as we got back home :) )
Stayed recently with my kids on the way to Qld. Great spot and great staff, would stay again on the next trip
overnight stay with the pup, comfortable, quiet enough, clean...handy for a snappy start to continue our journey. Parking was a bit tight.
We loved it. Awesome place ! Convenient location, comfy clean rooms and the staff we spoke to were both professional and friendly ! Thanks a tonne and will be back again.
Staff were awesome, cabin was very clean and comfortable!
Very noisy on the M1 highway

Very friendly greeting on checking in. Then offered an upgrade of cabin
The cabin was lovely & comfortable & very clean. The beds were very comfortable & the bathroom had the best hot shower
Even though the park is on the highway our cabin was very quiet.
They are very dog friendly which made us feel at home.
I would highly recommend Bannana coast caravan park Coffs Harbour. 👍👍👍👍
Tidy cabin, easy location and spot for us to stay on our travel down south

This place was clean and well equipped.  The main bed was just ok but I'm sure some would love it.  The bunks are awful. The floor was terrible, the lino tiles were all lifting off and sharp. I feel like the place is just being maintained and the owners dont really care. We stayed here 12 years ago and things haven’t changed.  This could be a really nice park.

Very basic but suited our needs fridge stove plates cutlery I enjoyed it and the staff were friendly. We used for sleeping and eating nice deck and clothes line too the swimming pool suited small children which was wonderful as well

Dog friendly,  on Highway Northen side , construction of freeway had diversion in place .
Staff friendly, didn't use pool or amneties as only a stop over .
Clean ,well presented .

Absolute horrible experience the manager of the park made myself and my sister feel very uncomfortable, she gave us very dirty looks from the moment we went into reception, very unprofessional and in my experience she is not quite adequate for a manager, i will be sure to let all my family and friends know to never stay at the Banana coast caravan park.

Had a great few days, location ok, a little bit out of town you need transport, buses run.
Accomodation clean and tidy cabin, good aircon, good shower.
Service was fantastic, lovely staff, friendly, happy to assist with needs.
Even posted back home my pillow I left behind.

We had a five star stay at this three star park. Chose a standard cabin as we were on a budget. Cabin was very clean and well presented. Everything we needed was supplied. Beds comfortable. AC kept the entire cabin cool. We stayed a week and were supplied with fresh towels etc every second day. Park staff very friendly and welcoming. Close to the Big Banana and shopping centre.

Essentially, you get what you pay for. $180 in total for a one night stay for 4 ppl. Relatively clean (asides from bathroom).

Definitely a bit dodgy though bunk bed close to coming off its hinges, shower head leaking, shower a bit dirty and in serious need of re-grouting and de-moulding.

Serious need of maintenance to everything, doors off their hinges and not closing properly, scratches, dents, marks on the walls (ironic with their “strict” cleanliness policy/statement).

All in all, definitely a one night stayer, couldn’t and wouldn’t stay more than one night.
Amazing stay! We stayed for only one night but the cabin was very very clean and tidy. We enjoyed it here

Cabins for guests are right against the road which is the Pacific Hwy! The noise is awful - day and night. We had to keep our doors & windows closed to try to block it out.
The cabins are clean but very small.
We stayed for a few nights to have a holiday in the area, but it is a one-night stay for a rest at best.
They allow pets which is handy but only recommend one night if you cant get elsewhere.

Great price and location for 3 star accommodation. Friendly staff well lit carpark besides the pot holes very clean and good presentation. Cabin was older but updated and was very clean and tidy no complaints. We enjoyed our stay and would recommend for a cheap stay or last minute accommodation.

Very comfortable cabin and dog friendly. Nice and clean and it was pleasant to walk around the park that is so beautifully maintained.  Car park was a tight squeeze for my car, but the young man doing the lawns was so helpful in guiding me out of it. A very lovely place to stay (but be careful with google map directions as it sends you across the road to then turn right onto the Princes Hwy which was too dangerous)  Best to do a u turn at the traffic lights pass the Big Banana
Very clean room ideal if U have a dog
Great location to get to all good beaches, clean and affordable
Fabulous, great cabin, clean toilet, great price. We'll be back.

Very friendly lady on check in.
Avg location and accommodation
Nice spacious cabin. Comfortable beds. Good location. Great friendly and helpful staff.

We stopped in for a few nights on our road trip up north and stayed in the delux cabin.

Bananacoast is conveniently located within a relatively close proximity to Coffs Harbour City Centre and within walking distance to The Big Banana.

The only other benefit is the size of the cabin we stayed in.

As soon as we walked in, the benefits started to dwindle, and we started to regret booking our 2 night stay. In short, the place was not clean or well kept.

In depth, walls filthy and dents everywhere, mould on the ceiling in the bathroom, urine smell coming from the lounge, and when we flipped the cushions, we noticed stains, sands, dust, dirt and hair underneath. There was a dampish odour, and the cutlery was worn and not clean. Cleaning them ourselves was hard as they provided an old washing up sponge. Curtains were filthy and damaged, and the bed was very hard and uncomfortable to sleep on.

I'd think twice about booking a cabin here. Camping or using your own caravan may be beneficial.

We had a fantastic stay. Very clean. Love the A/C that works properly. The site is very well maintained and an extra bonus that it's pet friendly.
The one and only little thing was that the wifi was very intermittent at times.
At no fault of this place, it is somewhat annoying to get out and into the city with the roadworks but it's close to the Big Banana, shopping plaza and plenty of restaurants.
Would highly recommended this place as we are planning a week long stay next time.
Great place very convenient & kid friendly.  Staff was amazing

We stayed here for 4 nights with 2 other couples in 2 cabins and a powered site. Cabins and powered site were close to the main highway and very noisy. Lots of permanent residents and those sites need tidying up and are too close to other cabins. We couldn’t open our cabin curtains because it looked straight into a permanent residents area. Our 2 cabins had a connecting verandah with not enough lighting to eat on the verandah. There was a step down in the middle of the verandah which was not marked to prevent injury. Wooden steps were rickety and car parking space was very tight. Our cabin was pretty basic, not very spacious and needs modernising.Not enough crockery, saucepans and cooking dishes. Air con/ heater had a stale smell when turned on.Powered site was right beside the highway and in a small gully so when it rained the outside awning mat was flooded.  BBQ area was being used as a building site so there were tradesmen tools, wheelbarrow and bags of cement in the BBQ area. The whole park has areas where building materials have been left lying around - gives the appearance of a scrap yard. Website states the park is a “short stroll to the beach” - but you have to put your life at risk getting across an extremely busy 4 lane highway first.  Locals that we spoke to didn’t have a very high opinion of this park which is pretty sad. On a positive note, the staff we dealt with were friendly. Would not recommend this park to others or stay here again.

Fantastic stay! Big room, very functional kitchen, easy to find and all for very cheap price. Me and my pet had a very good stay here, loved the staff and my stay. For a short stop hotel the place has everything, highly recommended.

We stayed here for a couple of nights and what we thought would be a wonderful tume turned out to be horrible. The staff were nice enough and we know our experience with the cabin itself is not their fault.
After the first night we started getting itchy & found that the lounge had bed bugs. 😞 The bathroom was dirty with hair on the roof as well as at the top of the shower we did mention something to staff. The oven had a horrible smell and when looking at it you could see food of some kind under it in view which was gross. The cabin had a horrible mouldy and musty smell which no matter what you did it would not go away and when you put the air con on to heat the cabin it had a horrible smell too.
All issues were addressed with management on site and we left a day early as we couldn't cope with the itching and finding bugs on us anymore.
Went to this caravan park. Amazing stuff. Love the environment. Will definitely come back again.
Great location! So close to the big banana. Facility were clean and comfortable. Affordable prices and amazing customer service! Would stay here again, honestly don’t you’d stay somewhere else.

Was a basic but clean cabin. Unfortunately due to the roadworks out the front it was noisy and took forever just to get across the road into coffs centre. Once the road works is complete it will be great. The location is good close to everything

Lovely staff
Nice cabins
Very affordable prices
I would stay there again
Nice caravan park very helpful and friendly receptionist

We stayed in a cabin. It was clean and quite comfortable. Nice staff .  The park is pet friendly so I could bring my little dog along  and is only a 2 minute drive from the shopping centre and 1 minute from the big banana. Loved the stay cabin had a few unclean spots but nothing to big

Whilst the receptionist was absolutely wonderful  the rest  of our experience was less than poor. The cabin was clean but sleeping arrangement was certainly not ideal Double bed in one room the second bedroom was a caged bunk . Had to ask the receptionist  to assist in taking the mattresses off the two bunks and throw them on the floor in the living area I am 81 years old and believe me that trying to get comfortable  on the floor is nearly impossible.  Very difficult to get up off such a low starting point. Sorry Bannanacoast Caravan park I will not be back .My travelling companion is disabled so she had preference of the beds . The noise of traffic pretty hard to cope with The cabin would probably suit a couple.Not. two single people. Cabin90. Right near the highway. Ended up sitting.up most of the night

Banana Coast Caravan Park. Pacific Highway Coffs Harbour

Booked another cabin for our trip home Wednesday 15/05/24.
Again The staff were very helpful and pleasant
Would like to thank Dan for the upgrade to a Villa. The rooms were very clean and tidy. Would recommend to anyone passing through to stay as it’s a great place and convenient for traveling North or South. It is also next door to the Big Banana on the pacific highway Coffs Harbour
Great staff. Nice clean cabins. Will definitely stay there again.

Faeces all over the amenities, please do not stay it is unhygienic and unsafe. I booked a powered site for 3 nights. The first night the amenities were filthy, faeces was in the hand basins, children's bath, in each of the 4 shower cubicles and all over the floor. It was too late to book somewhere else so we stayed one night with the hope staff would clean the facilities in the morning. To my pleasant surprise, they had cleaned the amenities early the next morning. However, when I went in for a shower before midday each cubicle still had faeces in it. I went to reception where the staff kindly stated they would clean it again and set down rodent traps. I returned to the amenities after 5 pm to find after 2 cleans by the cleaners there was still a lot of faeces on the floor and shower tap, see pictures below post cleaning. After this discovery, my partner and I contacted after hours and informed them we needed to check out and we requested a refund. We were informed that reception and staff on site have no authority to give refunds and that I must email the owner directly with my complaint and request.  Both myself and reception staff have emailed said owner multiple times over a 3-week period without a reply to either of us. The staff even attempted to phone while I was on-site but the owner's phone was turned off. I find the owner's treatment of staff and customers appalling. His park is run down, unhygienic and unfit for purpose. Had I known how filthy it was I would have never booked our stay. There are many cleaner safer caravan parks in Coffs for an extra $8, do yourself and favour and avoid at all costs.

Faeces all over the amenities, please do not stay it is unhygienic and unsafe. I booked a powered site for 3 nights. The first night the amenities were filthy, faeces were in the hand basins, children's bath, in each of the 4 shower cubicles and all over the floor, the men's amenities were in even worse shape. It was too late to book somewhere else so we stayed one night with the hope staff would clean the facilities in the morning. To my pleasant surprise they had cleaned the amenities early the next morning. However, when I went in for a shower before midday each cubicle still had faeces. I went to reception where the staff kindly stated they would clean it again and set down rodent traps. I returned to the amenities after 5pm to find after 2 cleans by the cleaners there was still a lot of faeces on the floor and shower tap, see pictures below which were taken post cleaning. After this discovery my partner and I contacted after hours and informed them we need to check out and we requested a refund. We were informed that reception and staff on site have no authority to give refunds, that I must email the owner directly with my complaint and request.  Both myself and reception staff have emailed said owner multiple times over a 3 week period without a reply to either of us. Staff even attempted to phone while I was onsite but the owner phone was turned off. I find the owners treatment of staff and customers appalling. His park is run down, unhygienic and unfit for purpose. Had I known how filthy it was I would have never booked our stay. There are many cleaner safer caravan parks in Coffs for an extra $8, do yourself and favour and avoid at all costs.
Wifi is a bit hit and miss.

The Bananacoast Caravan Park is located on the northern side of Coffs Harbour, just a short distance from the iconic Big Banana Fun Park. We stayed here for a few nights while we explored the Coffs Coast and it was sufficient for a short stay. Location wise you are right on the main Pacific Highway so it is busy 24hrs a day. In saying that, our air conditioned cabin was surprisingly quiet when the doors and windows were closed. The park itself was well maintained and the cabin was clean and in good order. The park had a great tennis court, pool and small playground plus a communal barbecue area. Overall I found the cabin on the higher side with regard to pricing (however I do understand I booked in peak season during the school holidays) plus I was slightly disappointed we didn't get any complimentary shampoo and body wash. This park also caters for pets I noticed for those travelling with fur babies 🐶🐾.

Best place we have stayed by far, Amazing customer service and great facilities.
Thank you😀😀
Really nice park.  Little cabin we stayed in was great for our needs.  Nice little verandah for us and the dog too!  All you need and nice and clean too.
Friendly and helpful staff, clean and comfortable rooms. Will definitely go there again.

Expensive, tiny, dirty cabins for a sleepover with dogs

Sheets didnt seem to be changed as there were some marks and cookie crumbs on one bunk bed that accompanied ants.

We all had to sleep on our own sleeping bag.

Booked for 2 nights camping, bailed after 1 night was kept awake all night by music and people drinking from people that must live there.
Kids didn't feel safe in that environment would never stay again!

Told the lady at desk about our experience all I got was a sorry no refund for the 2nd night we didn't stay.

Would never recommend anyone stay there. Wouldn't even give a star!
Good cabin with dog friendly.

We stayed here for one night at the beginning of our road trip and I’m so glad it was only for one night. If you have other options I would recommend taking them! It did the job, but not very pleasant in my opinion. Very old site, pool was dirty, a lot of people living there that just stare at you when you drive in, unpleasant amenities.
We stayed in a cabin. It was clean and quite comfortable. The park is fairly quiet and is only a 2 minute drive from the shopping centre and 1 minute from the big banana. Pet friendly is a bonus
Pet friendly, clean and quaint. Great for a stop over and super friendly staff
Friendly, centrally located, clean and  comfortable
Great service, will be our spot to stop on road trips from now on.
We got lots of highway 🛣 noise. But everything else  was great.

We where in desperate need of accommodation and they help us out straight away.

Belinda and Mel where amazing they helped us so much and where great with everything.

We will definitely be going back.

Good size room.
Bit of noise we were in cabin right at the front so could hear all the cars and trucks.
Staff FANTASTIC so friendly..
Perfect for a stop over when driving further north..
Would stay again..

The lady managing our booking and check in was delightfully friendly and helpful. We really appreciated the service.

The park itself is a tad run down. The location is quite noisy given the proximity to the trucks rumbling along the Pacific Highway all night. It's hard to know how noisy it is until you're there, I guess.

The cabin was okay, although the door was clunky. There were quite a few smokers in the adjacent cabins, which was a bit unpleasant. The cabins are tightly packed in.

Easily the most expensive cabin we stayed in on our holidays but also the most run-down.

Revolting amenities - filthy men's toilets at any time of day, just about anything you around the park is either rotten, rusted through or simply falling apart. The rails surrounding the childrens playground is no longer connected to the ground supports as the wooden support beams have rotted, cigarette stink and butts everywhere.  Nice friendly reception however this doesnt account for the mess this place is in. Very low budget for such a great area. Pity.

Budget cabin for $100 was filthy especially the couch, spring bed in a small tight room. The shower was so old it shot out luke warm water. We have stayed in far superior cabins for far lesser elsewhere and we travel alot. Avoid this horrible place.

We stayed 2 nights at Bananacoast Caravan Park. Seems to have limited powered sites (I managed to sight 4 lots only). We were sited next to the tennis court. Amenities do need tender loving care. The entrance/exit of the park is into a high pace road. Be careful when you are exiting as there are heaps of large vehicles travelling along the road. Quite a crowded park.
Needed some minor tender love on the bathroom door
細小的小屋，設備也算齊，但比較舊，所以價錢合理。
